prompt: Provide REAL per-channel CAC and sales-cycle benchmarks for selling a sub-$1,000 founder-led B2B security/compliance product to AI startups in 2026, with numbers not adjectives. For each channel give blended CAC (or cost-per-customer), typical sales-cycle length in days, and the source: (1) self-serve / PLG card-swipe via website; (2) founder cold outbound (email/LinkedIn/X DM); (3) YC/accelerator batch + founder Slack warm intro; (4) partner/marketplace listing (Vanta, Drata, MCP registry, AWS/GCP marketplace); (5) content/SEO inbound; (6) community (CISO Slacks, AI-founder X). Cite comparable companies' disclosed numbers (Vanta, Snyk, Sprinto, Pylon, early SOC2-automation tools) with URL/@handle+date. Then compute: at $750 one-time, which channels are CAC-positive and which are underwater, and what payback the $299/$999 mo continuous tier needs to rescue an underwater channel. Rank channels by CAC-efficiency for the first 100 customers; flag confidence and where CAC is estimated.
content:
**Limited public disclosures exist for exact per-channel CAC on sub-$1,000 founder-led B2B security/compliance tools targeting AI startups (or close analogs like Vanta, Snyk, Sprinto).** Most comparables emphasize overall efficiency, sales cycles, or high-level GTM rather than granular channel CAC. Vanta (compliance automation, heavy YC/startup focus) reports strong sales efficiency and short cycles but no public CAC breakdowns. Sprinto (similar compliance automation) highlights early customer acquisition via networks/VC partnerships but no numeric CAC. Snyk and Pylon lack disclosed channel-specific figures in public sources.[[1]](https://www.saastr.com/the-early-days-5-things-vanta-got-right-and-5-it-got-wrong-getting-to-first-10m-arr/)[[2]](https://www.unusual.vc/how-vanta-found-product-market-fit-christina-cacioppo-on-startup-compliance/)[[3]](https://saas.group/podcasts/saas-unbound-interview-girish-redekar-sprinto/)

Benchmarks below draw from 2025–2026 B2B SaaS industry data (hundreds of companies, focused on SMB/self-serve or low-ACV deals relevant to sub-$1k products), adjusted for security/compliance verticals where possible (higher intent but regulatory friction). These are blended or channel-specific where reported; pure founder-led early-stage (pre-scale) often sees lower CAC than mature companies due to founder time leverage. Sales cycles are typical for AI/startup buyers (fast decisions, less bureaucracy). Sources noted; many are estimates interpolated from ranges (e.g., SMB self-serve $200–700 CAC).[[4]](https://ltvcacbook.com/blog/cac-benchmarks-2026)[[5]](https://www.saashero.net/strategy/saas-cac-benchmarks-2026/)[[6]](https://www.digitalapplied.com/blog/customer-acquisition-cost-benchmarks-2026-industry)[[7]](https://www.factors.ai/blog/customer-acquisition-cost)

### Channel Benchmarks (Blended CAC or Cost-per-Customer, Sales Cycle in Days)
1. **Self-serve / PLG card-swipe via website**  
   Blended CAC: $200–600 (median ~$340–500 for pure self-serve/SMB; lower end for high-intent compliance tools).  
   Sales cycle: 1–14 days (often instant or demo-to-close).  
   Source/Notes: 2026 SaaS benchmarks for self-serve/PLG; Vanta-like tools see fast conversion on website trials. Estimated for founder-led (no paid ads initially). High confidence on range.[[6]](https://www.digitalapplied.com/blog/customer-acquisition-cost-benchmarks-2026-industry)[[7]](https://www.factors.ai/blog/customer-acquisition-cost)

2. **Founder cold outbound (email/LinkedIn/X DM)**  
   Blended CAC: $400–2,000 (often $1,000–1,980 fully loaded with tools/time; lower for targeted founder effort at small scale).  
   Sales cycle: 30–90 days (reply-to-close; 3–4% reply rates typical).  
   Source/Notes: Industry outbound benchmarks; founder-led can compress via personalization but scales poorly. Comparable to early Sprinto/SOC 2 tools. Medium confidence; highly variable by volume/response.[[8]](https://www.olivermunro.com/writersblog/saas-marketing-statistics)[[9]](https://prospeo.io/s/customer-acquisition-metrics)

3. **YC/accelerator batch + founder Slack warm intro**  
   Blended CAC: $50–300 (often near $0–150 beyond founder time; warm intros convert at high rates).  
   Sales cycle: 7–30 days (very high close rates from trust).  
   Source/Notes: Early Sprinto/YC-adjacent tools leveraged batches/networks for first customers; Vanta noted strong YC cohort penetration. Estimated low end for founder networks. High confidence on relative efficiency.[[3]](https://saas.group/podcasts/saas-unbound-interview-girish-redekar-sprinto/)

4. **Partner/marketplace listing (Vanta, Drata, MCP registry, AWS/GCP marketplace)**  
   Blended CAC: $300–800 (referral/partner share or listing fees; lower effective via co-sell).  
   Sales cycle: 14–60 days (intent-driven but integration/review time).  
   Source/Notes: Partner/referral benchmarks (~$150–500 in aggregates); Vanta/Drata ecosystems drive inbound for compliance tools. Estimated; partnerships often cited by Sprinto. Medium confidence.[[9]](https://prospeo.io/s/customer-acquisition-metrics)

5. **Content/SEO inbound**  
   Blended CAC: $480–942 (organic; drops long-term to ~$290–500 as content compounds).  
   Sales cycle: 30–90 days (research-to-purchase for compliance).  
   Source/Notes: 2025–2026 channel benchmarks for SEO/content in B2B SaaS; relevant for security thought leadership. Medium-high confidence on range.[[8]](https://www.olivermunro.com/writersblog/saas-marketing-statistics)

6. **Community (CISO Slacks, AI-founder X)**  
   Blended CAC: $100–400 (near-zero direct cost; time + organic amplification).  
   Sales cycle: 7–45 days (high trust/virality in founder communities).  
   Source/Notes: Referral/community benchmarks (~$150); common for early AI/security tools via X/Slacks. Estimated low for targeted founder presence. Medium confidence.[[9]](https://prospeo.io/s/customer-acquisition-metrics)

**Vanta-specific notes**: ~16-day average sales cycle for startup deals (sometimes 1-call close); longer (70–90+ days or 6 months) for enterprise. Strong overall sales efficiency metrics reported but not channel-broken.[[2]](https://www.unusual.vc/how-vanta-found-product-market-fit-christina-cacioppo-on-startup-compliance/)[[10]](https://gtmnow.com/gtm-158-from-startup-to-4b-unicorn-vanta-cro-stevie-case/)

### Analysis at $750 One-Time Price
- **CAC-positive channels** (CAC < $750): Self-serve/PLG (1), YC/warm intros (3), community (6), and often partner/marketplace (4) and content/SEO (5) at efficient execution.  
- **Underwater channels** (CAC > $750 possible): Cold outbound (2) frequently underwater at scale or with poor response rates; higher-end estimates for content/SEO or partners if not optimized.  
Payback via $299/mo or $999/mo continuous tier: Underwater channels need recurring revenue to recover. At $299/mo (assume ~80–90% gross margin after costs): ~3–7 months payback to cover $1,000–2,000 CAC (e.g., 4–7 months at $299/mo for $1,200 CAC). At $999/mo: 1–3 months payback (much faster rescue). Blended LTV:CAC targets of 3:1+ are standard; shorter payback (<12 months ideal) rescues marginal channels. Self-serve benefits most from one-time upsell to recurring.[[4]](https://ltvcacbook.com/blog/cac-benchmarks-2026)

### Ranking by CAC-Efficiency for First 100 Customers (Founder-Led Context)
1. YC/accelerator + warm intros (lowest CAC, fastest closes via trust).  
2. Community (organic, high conversion in AI/startup circles).  
3. Self-serve/PLG (scalable, low variable cost once site converts).  
4. Partner/marketplace (intent + co-marketing leverage).  
5. Content/SEO (compounding but slower ramp).  
6. Founder cold outbound (highest CAC, time-intensive; best as supplement).  

**Confidence flags**: Ranges are directionally robust from 2026 benchmarks but not product-specific (no exact Vanta/Sprinto/Snyk disclosures found); early founder-led often beats averages via networks. CAC estimated where channel data is aggregated rather than exact. Sales cycles more reliable from Vanta analogs. Update with your own tracking (e.g., via CRM attribution) as vertical intent for compliance can compress CAC vs. generic SaaS.
